titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Harry H. Ensign Autograph Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by Ruth Gay

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 28, 2020 at 07:02

did.abstract: Miscellaneous autographs and letters of American political and cultural figures, among them John and John Quincy Adams, Daniel Webster, Jefferson Davis, Horace Greeley, and Lydia Sigourney. Also included are eleven letters to James F. Babcock, editor of the New Haven Weekly Palladium , on politics (1840-1866) and seven letters (1866-1872) to R. P. Cowles in New Haven from prospective lecturers.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Harry H. Ensign Autograph Collection (MS 1228). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous autographs and letters of American political and cultural figures, among them John and John Quincy Adams, Daniel Webster, Jefferson Davis, Horace Greeley, and Lydia Sigourney. Also included are eleven letters to James F. Babcock, editor of the New Haven Weekly Palladium , on politics (1840-1866) and seven letters (1866-1872) to R. P. Cowles in New Haven from prospective lecturers.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

did.unittitle: Adams, John (1735-1826). Thanking Major General Dearborn "for introducing to me, Mr. Binon whom I find to be a gentleman of Sense and Letters..."
Adams, John Quincy (1767-1848). Declining an invitation of the Whigs of New Haven to attend a 4th of July celebration.

Brown, John, Jr. From John Brown, Jr. to Harry H. Ensign, a hardware dealer in New Haven, complying with his request for an autograph of John Brown. The reply is written on the verso of Ensign's letter. The autograph (enclosed) was cut from a letter by John Brown to his son.

Lyon, Nathaniel (1818-1861). To his nephew, John A. Hosler in Webster, Mass. From Fort Riley, Kansas Territory with reminiscences on his army career, comments on the prohibition of slavery in Kansas, and on family news. [4] p.

Mitchell, Donald Grant (1822-1908). To "My dear Madam" from Edgewood [New Haven] offering publishing advice. "Occasional letters upon rural topics, having practical interest, and of a piquant style are always in place." 1 p.
Sigourney, Lydia Howard Huntley (1791-1865). To "My dear Sir" asking for his emendations on my 'Boy's Book" 1 p.
Tyler, John, Pres. U.S. (1790-1862). To President Harrison recommending Richard Adams for appointment as Consul to Havana. Written less than one month before Tyler became President. 1 p.
To Porter, Noah (1811-1892). Re: Chamber of Commerce
